data.title: Nvidia Stock Has a Powerful Catalyst Coming. Analyst Says Blackwell Superclusters Will Be "a Full Generation Ahead of Competition"
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) stock has been stuck in a trading range for the last month, falling about 4.9% and heading for small weekly losses. Shareholders who are waiting for a change might have to wait a little longer, but a major event in early 2026 could be the powerful spark needed to drive the stock higher.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

The main problems weighing on the stock are simple worries about the overall AI market and growing competition. Broadcom (AVGO), a key competitor, saw its stock drop even after good earnings because investors wanted more news about new AI chip customers. More importantly, Google's (GOOGL) Tensor Processing Units (TPUs), custom chips designed for AI, are seen as a rising threat to Nvidia's dominance.

Why Blackwell Is Nvidia's Catalyst

The biggest hope for shaking Nvidia out of its sideways trading is the expected rollout of new AI models. These cutting-edge models are being trained on the company's latest, most powerful hardware: the Nvidia Blackwell NVL72 superclusters.

Bank of America Securities analyst Vivek Arya believes these new, major model releases will be the key factor. "Blackwell-based models should begin to roll out starting early 2026, by the time when Nvidia believes it would be clear that they are at least a full generation ahead of competition," Arya wrote in a recent note.

The analyst gave Nvidia an "Overweight" rating and set a $275 price target on the stock, arguing that the release of these new Blackwell-based models could change the momentum and settle the debate against Google's TPUs.

Nvidia Aims for the Best GPU Architecture

Google's TPUs are custom chips designed specifically for AI training and inference. They are less flexible than Nvidia's GPUs but are highly efficient for Google's own AI work. The success of powerful new Google models, like Gemini 3, has shown the industry that non-Nvidia hardware can achieve top-tier results, causing worries about Nvidia's near-monopoly.

Therefore, the upcoming Blackwell-trained models are not just a normal product refresh; they are a necessary show of strength to prove that Nvidia remains a "full generation ahead" and that its powerful GPU architecture is still the best and most necessary hardware for the world's most advanced AI research.

Is Nvidia a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

Analyst sentiment for Nvidia (NVDA) is highly bullish, maintaining a consensus rating of Strong Buy. This is based on ratings from 41 Wall Street analysts in the last three months, with 39 analysts recommending a Buy, one recommending a Hold, and one recommending a Sell. The average 12-month consensus price target for NVDA is $258.45. This average target represents a substantial 42.9% upside from the last closing price.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Why Is Jiuzi Stock (JZXN) Up Today?
data.description:
Jiuzi (JZXN) stock rocketed higher on Friday after the new energy vehicle retail operator announced a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) to increase its private placement size to up to $1 billion. This MOU was reached with several institutional investors and is an expansion of the $12 million private placement plan that was revealed on October 7, 2025.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Jiuzi said that the funds from this private placement will be used to expand its crypto asset business, including building advanced secure custody infrastructure and innovative storage solutions. The company noted that this will allow it to capitalize on the growing market demand for crypto asset services.

Tao Li, CEO of Jiuzi, said, "The strong trust placed by investors validates our strategic vision and execution capabilities. This substantial additional capital provides us with significant financial flexibility to build secure crypto asset custody infrastructure and pursue strategic acquisition opportunities within the rapidly evolving crypto asset storage ecosystem."

Jiuzi Stock Movement Today

Jiuzi stock was up 44.4% in pre-market trading on Friday, following a 6.04% dip yesterday. The stock has also decreased 89.9% year-to-date and 92.09% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came strong early-morning trading of JZXN stock, as more than 5.5 million shares changed hands. For comparison, the stock's three-month daily average trading volume is about 7 million units.

There's a lack of analyst coverage for Jiuzi, meaning traders will want to be careful about making an investment in the company. That's doubly true considering its volatile nature and the large drops experienced in 2025.
data.author: William White

data.title: HOOD, IBTA, IRDM: Cathie Wood Buys the Dip in Robinhood, Sells Ibotta and Iridium Stocks
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) reported several key trades for Thursday, December 11. The prominent hedge fund manager capitalized on the 9% drop in Robinhood Markets (HOOD) shares, an online brokerage firm, due to weak November trading volumes. At the same time, the firm continued to trim positions across several innovation-focused names, including Ibotta (IBTA) and Iridium Communications (IRDM).

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Wood Increases Exposure in Robinhood Stock

ARK's largest move of the day was a strong buy in Robinhood. The firm bought 124,427 shares worth about $16.9 million through ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) and the ARK Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW). This follows a smaller buy on December 5 and signals rising confidence in Robinhood as user activity and new products gain traction.

Robinhood reported a sharp slowdown in November activity: equity trading volumes fell 37%, options dropped 28%, and crypto slipped 12%. Weaker markets also pushed assets under custody down 5% to $325 billion, marking the first decline since February. Even so, the firm still posted solid net deposits of $7.1 billion, up 27% from October. 

On TipRanks, Robinhood's stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 20 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 14 Buy, five Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average HOOD price target of $151.39 implies 22.57% upside from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: NVDA Stock Forecast: Analysts Say Nvidia Could Hit $258, Up 43% from Current Levels
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) stock remains in the spotlight amid growing concerns over an artificial intelligence (AI) bubble. The chipmaker's shares have already surged nearly 35% in 2025, and Wall Street sees another 43% upside over the next twelve months. Nvidia bulls continue to dominate bears, fueled by major catalysts like the AI data center boom, new GPU (graphics processing unit) launches, strong earnings momentum, and market expansion. Yet some analysts argue the shares are fairly valued given this growth potential.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

On TipRanks, NVDA stock commands a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 39 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell rating. Also, the average Nvidia price target of $258.45 implies 42.8% upside potential from current levels. Let's understand some analysts' views in detail.

Analysts Hail Nvidia as the AI King

BofA Securities' top analyst Vivek Arya has a Buy rating and $275 price target, implying 52% upside potential. He noted that NVDA GPUs lead competitors by a full generation. Blackwell offers 10-15x better model training, while Vera Rubin is slated for second half of 2026. Arya pointed to clear demand visibility for at least $500 billion in cumulative sales over 2025-26, with Google (GOOGL) as a key, growing customer and nearly every major model builder still relying on NVDA.

Similarly, Wells Fargo analyst Aaron Rakers reaffirmed his $265 price target, implying 46.5% upside potential after the White House lifted the ban on export of H200 chips to China. Rakers estimates this unlocks $25 to $30 billion in annual revenue and adds $0.60 to $0.70 to earnings per share.

Nvidia Faces Rising Chip Competition

In contrast, Seaport Global analyst Jay Goldberg reiterated his Sell rating and $140 price target, implying 22.6% downside potential. He believes Nvidia faces rising competition and is using various sales tactics to cope, which are not yet fully visible in financials but are already significant and expected to grow in 2026. He noted that Alphabet (GOOGL) has done a strong job getting outside customers to use its in-house TPUs (tensor processing units). He added that although TPUs are a niche fit, they can beat Nvidia systems on several performance measures.

Is NVDA Stock a Buy?

Wall Street rates NVDA a Strong Buy, with 43% upside from current levels.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 12/12/25 – U.S. Stock Futures Mixed as Investors Move Out of AI Plays
data.description:
U.S. stock futures traded mixed early Friday, after investors shifted from artificial intelligence (AI) stocks to other categories in the broader market following Oracle's (ORCL) fiscal second-quarter results. The database software and cloud computing company missed revenue expectations and sparked concerns about elevated AI spending. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) were down 0.21%, while futures tied to the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.03% and 0.29%, respectively, at 4:14 a.m. EST on December 12.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

During Thursday's regular trading session, the Dow Jones and the S&P 500 rose 1.34% and 0.21%, respectively, reaching record highs. Meanwhile, the Nasdaq Composite fell 0.26% as investors moved away from high-flying technology stocks.

Meanwhile, shares of chip company Broadcom (AVGO) were down 4.1% in Friday's pre-market trading despite reporting better-than-expected earnings for the fourth quarter of Fiscal 2025. Meanwhile, AI stocks, including Oracle, Nvidia (NVDA), and CoreWeave (CRWV), continued to trend lower today. Also, Lululemon (LULU) stock rallied 10% after the athleisure company announced better-than-expected Q3 results and CEO departure.

Turning to economic releases, investors await a slate of Fed speeches that could provide key insights into the U.S. economy.

The U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.168%. WTI crude oil futures were trending higher, hovering near $58.05 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price increased to nearly $4,312 per ounce.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened higher today as record sessions for two major U.S. indexes boosted investor sentiment.

Asia-Pacific Markets Close Higher

Asia-Pacific markets closed higher following new records touched by the Dow Jones and the S&P 500, as well as the recent 25-basis-point interest rate cut by the U.S. Federal Reserve.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index closed 1.75% higher. In China, the Shanghai Composite rose 0.41%, while the Shenzhen Component was up 0.84%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei gained 1.37%, and the Topix rose 1.98%.

data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Here Are 7 Space Stocks Poised to Rally as Jeff Bezos & Elon Musks' AI Orbital Rivalry Continues
data.description:
The escalating rivalry between Elon Musk and Jeff Bezos to deploy AI data centers in orbit is redirecting massive capital expenditure and creating a new investing roadmap for Earthly stocks. The central driver is solving the power constraint facing AI expansion; orbital solar power is seen as a way to scale computation far beyond the limits of Earth's electrical grid.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

This "compute in orbit" narrative is creating investment opportunities across two key categories: the specialized Space Infrastructure needed for the satellites, and the Terrestrial Grid Technology that will still dominate near-term AI spending.

Sector 1: Space Infrastructure Plays Are Poised to Launch

The initial building phases of orbital AI will immediately boost companies that supply the components and launch services, validating a stock rally in these names.

Rocket Lab (RKLB) is a primary winner. As a leading launch provider, the stock is currently rated a "Moderate Buy" with an average 12-month target of $65.17, benefiting from the potential boom in missions required to deploy these new AI-capable satellites.

Redwire (RDW), the "nuts and bolts" supplier of space-qualified components, also carries a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with analysts targeting $11.88, though its high volatility (Beta of 2.49) reflects the inherent risks of the aerospace sector.

Finally, Microchip Technology (MCHP), rated a "Moderate Buy" with an average price target of $72.73, is a direct way to play the demand for space-grade, radiation-tolerant silicon, which is essential for the extreme environment of orbital AI computing.

Sector 2: Terrestrial Grid & Power Plays Offer Immediate Upside

Even with the space focus, analysts agree that the "dominant spend path" for billions in capital remains the massive power infrastructure buildout on Earth. This guarantees a strong rally in "Grid Tech" stocks.

Eaton (ETN) sits at the heart of the AI power story, managing the distribution systems for high-density data centers. The stock has a "Strong Buy" consensus with an average target of $425.25, representing a potential 21.4% upside from its current price.

Vertiv (VRT), a leader in critical power and cooling infrastructure, is also rated a "Strong Buy" with an average target of $203.25, fueled by the immense heat generated by AI servers. Its earnings are forecast to grow robustly at over 24% per year.

Supporting these leaders are companies like Hubbell (HUBB) and Quanta Services (PWR), which provide the essential electrical components and physical construction services, respectively, to modernize the grid and handle the huge new data center load. Quanta Services is rated a " Moderate Buy" with an average price target of $466.91, offering visibility into strong future growth from large projects.

Investors can compare all these stocks side-by-side based on various financial metrics and analyst ratings on the TipRanks Stocks Comparison Tool. Click on the image below to explore the tool.
data.author: Annika Masrani
